action.reasoning: Observation: Best test IC is 0.016978 from ensemble_mlp_v2. I have 1.01 hours and 5 iterations left. The last submission confirmed ensemble_mlp_v2 is still the best. Analysis: I've tried many approaches and ensemble_mlp_v2 remains the best. With only 5 iterations left and ~1 hour, I should try one more training run to see if I can beat it, then finalize. The key insight: ensemble_mlp_v2 used specific hyperparameters that worked well. Let me try to train a new ensemble that combines the best models from v2 with additional models trained with slightly different hyperparameters. Actually, looking at the time constraints, I should try one more training run with the exact same approach as v2 but with more models and slightly better hyperparameters. The v2 used train_ensemble_mlp.py. Let me try to replicate it with more models and slightly different seeds. Decision: Train one more ensemble with the same successful approach as v2 (MSE loss, val on 2021, similar architectures) but with 10 models and slightly different hyperparameters. If it doesn't beat v2, submit v2 as final.
